task_completion.description: FreeCAD is opened and ready for modeling.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD application window is visible on the screen.
A Box object named 'Arm_A_Workspace' is visible in the Tree View (Model tab). If selected, its dimensions in the Property View should be 80mm x 60mm x 50mm.

task_completion.description: The second box, 'Arm_B_Workspace', is created and positioned.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Box object named 'Arm_B_Workspace' is visible in the Tree View. In the 3D view, two distinct but overlapping rectangular boxes should be visible.

task_completion.description: The Boolean intersection (Common) of the two boxes is computed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Common' or 'Intersection' feature is visible in the Tree View, and the original 'Arm_A_Workspace' and 'Arm_B_Workspace' boxes are nested underneath it as its children. The 3D view should show a single, smaller rectangular block representing the overlapping volume.

task_completion.description: The result is saved to the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD window title bar displays 'workspace_intersection' or a save dialog confirms the file is being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/workspace_intersection.FCStd'.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeCAD GUI to perform the modeling tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through FreeCAD menus, toolbars, or the Tree View, rather than just executing terminal commands or running a background script.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed a Boolean intersection rather than faking the final shape.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Tree View must show a Boolean operation (e.g., 'Common') that contains the two original boxes as children. The agent should not simply create a single new box with the final dimensions (40x40x50) to mimic the result.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual FreeCAD application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD interface is clearly visible and interacted with, not a text editor or a Python console bypassing the main application UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Box 1 (Arm_A_Workspace) must be 80x60x50 mm at origin (0,0,0). Box 2 (Arm_B_Workspace) must be 70x50x50 mm at position (40,20,0). The resulting Boolean intersection must visually and mathematically be a single block with dimensions 40x40x50 mm (volume: 80,000 mm³). The final file must be saved exactly as /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/workspace_intersection.FCStd.
